b:T38f7,<div class="specification">
<p>The graph shows the change in the membrane potential of an axon during an action potential.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State the approximate value of the membrane potential at X.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Y is the threshold potential. State what happens when the threshold potential is reached.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Describe the movements in ions that occur during time <em>t</em>.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Explain how a nerve impulse is passed on to other neurons.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">d.</div>
</div>c:T6dc,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>0 mV; (<em>accept answers in the range of – 10 mV to +10 mV</em>) (<em>Units required</em>)</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>sodium channels (start to) open<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>depolarization/axon begins to depolarize<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>action potential occurs;</p>
<p><em>Reject pumping of ions</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Na<sup>+</sup>/sodium ions diffuse into the axon (in the first part/half of <em>t</em>);<br/>K<sup>+</sup> /potassium ions diffuse out of the axon (in the second half/part of <em>t</em>)</p>
<p><em>Do not accept the name of the element without indication that it is an ion</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<ol style="list-style-type:lower-alpha;">
<li>impulses pass to another neuron at a <span style="text-decoration:underline;">synapse</span>/<span style="text-decoration:underline;">across synaptic gap</span>/<span style="text-decoration:underline;">cleft</span>;</li>
<li>(depolarization causes) Ca<sup>2+</sup>/calcium ions to diffuse into the (presynaptic) neuron/axon;</li>
<li>depolarization (of presynaptic neuron) causes release of a neurotransmitter<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>neurotransmitters diffuse across the synapse;</li>
<li>(neurotransmitters) bind to receptors on postsynaptic neuron/membrane;</li>
<li>(if the threshold potential is reached) an action potential occurs/sodium gates open (in the postsynaptic neuron);</li>
</ol>
<div class="question_part_label">d.</div>
</div>d:T492,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>There were some truly brilliant answers to Question 5. However, the weakest answers in the examination were also found here. Of the questions, these were the most commonly left blank.</p>
<p>Candidates were often unable to approximate X. Missing units resulted in no marks awarded.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Fundamental terms such as action potential or depolarization were not used in Q5(b) and (c). Some candidates failed to distinguish between elements and ions.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Some candidates talked about ion movement but with no reference to a specific ion; some described the Na<sup>+</sup>/K<sup>+</sup> pump here and received no marks.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Use of term "synapse" was often missing; involvement of the brain was sometimes built into the passage of a nerve impulse to another neuron.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">d.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/6/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","6"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"22M.2.SL.TZ1.5"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"22M"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","6-5-neurons-and-synapses"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$d"}}]]}]]}]
